tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine and microalbumin/creatinine ratio, indicating significant renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, bilirubinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine color/appearance, supporting renal dysfunction. Mild anemia is present. HbA1c is low, suggesting no chronic hyperglycemia. No evidence of infection. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation for possible acute or chronic kidney disease and further assessment of underlying etiology.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
